summary: Dashboard hardcodes MAX_TOTAL_POSITIONS=10 with comment "must match backend config" creating drift risk if backend changes
Backtest UI requires dates but does not validate start<=end before submitting to backend
Broad `except Exception: pass` in DB migration blocks can mask unexpected migration failures
`/api/system/status` may return default values after subsystem failures unless caller inspects `check_errors`
Trading process state can temporarily diverge across in-memory registry/system_state/profiles.status during crash-restart windows
Train endpoint forces `years=6` for all model types when request omits override
status: overriding scalp trainer's internal 2-year default and potentially inflating training runtime/data scope

summary: `_active_jobs` slot is claimed before `thread.start()` and there is no rollback if thread creation/start fails
status: which can leave profile stuck as "in progress"

summary: Trade logging derives model_type from predictor class name (`scalp`/`swingclassifier`) which may not match canonical DB model_type values (`xgb_classifier`/`xgb_swing_classifier`/`lgbm_classifier`)
On predictor load error strategy falls back to `XGBoostPredictor` using same model path
status: risking model-family bypass if intended predictor is non-xgboost

summary: _detect_model_type defaults to `xgboost` when DB lookup fails which can misclassify inference semantics under metadata failure
`step_stopped_at` values are overloaded (same step value used by multiple independent rejection reasons)
status: making direct gate-count interpretation ambiguous without stop_reason stratification

summary: Step `12` is used as entered-success marker (entered=true) while still encoded in `step_stopped_at`
status: which can distort naive "kill-count by step" analyses

summary: Current snapshot lacks runtime replay evidence needed to elevate findings from structural confidence to operational certainty
